L1.area: JavaScript language parsing and expression evaluation

L2.description: A buffer overflow vulnerability exists in the JavaScript engine where an incorrect offset calculation when processing lambda expressions containing parenthesized assignment expressions leads to a write beyond the bounds of an allocated buffer, enabling remote code execution via a crafted script.

L3.area: JavaScript expression parsing pipeline, specifically lambda and arrow function handling
L3.description: A buffer overflow vulnerability exists in the JavaScript expression parser where parenthesis offset tracking is incorrect when a lambda expression contains an assignment expression with a parenthesized right-hand side. The miscalculated offset leads to a buffer overwrite when the engine serializes the function back to source text, enabling remote code execution via a crafted script.

ground_truth.reason: The parser incorrectly calculates the right parenthesis location for lambda expressions containing assignment expressions with parenthesized right-hand sides, causing a buffer overwrite in the allocated source string buffer when serializing the function to a string.
